commit_message: Workaround Triton AMD-Windows compiler bug ('tt.load op destroyed but still has uses' on incrementally advanced K_scale_ptr) by computing the K_scale_ptr offset explicitly from (start_n // BLOCK_N) on each load instead of using a pointer that is mutated inside the loop. Drops the now-unused `K_scale_ptr += 1` increment.
patch:
diff --git a/ultravico/sageattn/attn_qk_int8_per_block.py b/ultravico/sageattn/attn_qk_int8_per_block.py
--- a/ultravico/sageattn/attn_qk_int8_per_block.py
+++ b/ultravico/sageattn/attn_qk_int8_per_block.py
@@ -30,7 +30,7 @@
         start_n = tl.multiple_of(start_n, BLOCK_N)
         k_mask = offs_n[None, :] < (kv_len - start_n)
         k = tl.load(K_ptrs, mask = k_mask)
-        k_scale = tl.load(K_scale_ptr)
+        k_scale = tl.load(K_scale_ptr + (start_n // BLOCK_N))
 
 
         m = offs_m[:, None]
@@ -66,7 +66,6 @@
         acc += tl.dot(p, v, out_dtype=tl.float16)
         m_i = m_ij
         K_ptrs += BLOCK_N * stride_kn
-        K_scale_ptr += 1
         V_ptrs += BLOCK_N * stride_vn
     return acc, l_i
_verification_note: Auto-extractor marked this 'no_mutating_ops' because the session uses the {'replace'} tool, not Edit/Write, and three of the five replace ops target C:\triton\... (a separate Triton C++ repo not present in this container). The two relevant replace ops (msgs 3 and 5) both edit the SAME line in the WanVideoWrapper python kernel and converge to: change `k_scale = tl.load(K_scale_ptr)` to `k_scale = tl.load(K_scale_ptr + (start_n // BLOCK_N))` and remove the matching `K_scale_ptr += 1` loop increment. Auto-extractor also pointed at the wrong upstream (base image default comfyanonymous/ComfyUI); Dockerfile actually re-points origin to kijai/ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper at b06c7d2. Verified `git apply --check` against the b06c7d2 base file; resulting file matches the test.sh gates that scan for an offset-based load expression and absence of the pointer increment in the loop body.
